_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: MA LG Driscoll, MD LG Miller, MD Comptroller Lierman, ME AG Frey, MI AG Nessel. All sitting Democratic statewide execs with real records. Evidence-first; every FALSE cited; null where no evidence; cross-party credit where earned (Frey opposed 2021 drug decriminalization -> public_justice TRUE).

kim-driscoll.notes_set: MA Lt. Governor (Healey-Driscoll admin) since Jan 2023; former Salem mayor 2006-2023. Launched 2026 reelection campaign Jan 20, 2026. Consistent progressive across all 10 rubric axes; no cross-party deviation found. Pro-abortion ('abortion care is healthcare'; PP Advocacy Fund of MA endorsement). LGBTQ ally (100% HRC; reaffirmed gender-affirming-care protections incl. minors). HIGH-VALUE: as Salem mayor in 2014 terminated Gordon College (Christian college) contract to manage Old Town Hall after it sought a religious exemption from an LGBT-nondiscrimination order ('religious freedom does not afford you the right to impose those beliefs upon others') -> documented refusal of Christian conscience exemption. Backed MA 2024 firearms law (AWB, magazine ban, expanded red-flag). Admin pushed keep-ICE-out legislation (de facto sanctuary) and ESG-style green bank. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Pro-abortion-rights; 'abortion care is healthcare.' Does not affirm life/personhood from conception.

1.note: Administration expanded abortion access (emergency reproductive-health regs, $1.8M access funding) - opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocacy Fund of MA for reelection.

0.note: LGBTQ ally; 100% HRC Municipal Equality Index as Salem mayor. Does not affirm marriage as man-woman only.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage / LGBTQ legal equality.

2.note: Administration reaffirmed gender-affirming-care protections incl. minors -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

1.note: Protects gender-affirming care for minors -> contrary to parental notification/consent on minors' gender interventions.
As Salem mayor (2014) terminated Gordon College (Christian college) contract after it sought a religious conscience exemption from an LGBT-nondiscrimination order.

4.note: Administration created the nation's first green bank / ESG-style climate finance -> contrary to opposing ESG capture. (SOFT)

1.note: Administration filed keep-ICE-out legislation; opposes mass deportation enforcement.

2.note: Keep-ICE-out posture (schools/hospitals/courthouses/churches), ICE-misconduct portal -> de facto sanctuary.

0.note: Backed MA 2024 firearms law -> opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Backed MA 2024 law: assault-weapons & large-capacity-magazine ban, expanded red-flag/ERPO.

aruna-miller.notes_set: MD Lt. Governor (Moore-Miller admin) since Jan 2023; former MD House of Delegates (Montgomery Co., Dist. 15, 2010-2019). Filed for 2026 reelection. Pro-abortion: championed the 2024 Reproductive Freedom Amendment ('protecting future generations of women with a constitutional right to choose'); EMILY's List endorsed her twice. Voted FOR the 2012 Civil Marriage Protection Act (same-sex marriage). Backed the Trans Health Equity Act / gender-affirming-care EO. Defended MD mail-in voting (2026), rejecting Trump's fraud claims. Supports gun control (assault-weapons ban, universal background checks; appeared with Moms/Students Demand Action). NOTE: the indexed 'Maryland is not going to become a sanctuary state' quote belongs to the late Senate President Mike Miller, NOT Aruna Miller - she has no documented personal immigration position, so immigration cells left null. No cross-party deviation found. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Championed 2024 Reproductive Freedom Amendment; pro-choice. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocated to protect/expand abortion rights -> opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List (twice) - the abortion-rights candidate fund.

0.note: Voted FOR the 2012 Civil Marriage Protection Act legalizing same-sex marriage in MD.

1.note: Supported same-sex marriage.

2.note: Backed Trans Health Equity Act and gender-affirming-care EO -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Public advocacy for trans/LGBTQ protections in policy.

2.note: Defended MD's mail-in voting system (2026) -> contrary to single-day in-person-only voting.

3.note: Defended mass mail-in voting; rejected calls to restrict it.

0.note: 2018 platform: assault-weapons ban + universal background checks; as LG part of admin that signed 3 gun-control bills -> opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports assault-weapons ban and stricter gun regulation.

brooke-lierman.notes_set: MD Comptroller (34th; first woman elected to the office) since Jan 2023; former MD House of Delegates (Baltimore City, Dist. 46, 2015-2023). Announced 2026 reelection Sept 17, 2025; GOP opponent Sonya Dunn. Pro-abortion: co-sponsored + voted for the 2022 Abortion Care Access Act (HB937), floor advocate for the reproductive-liberty amendment (HB1171); former Planned Parenthood intern and NARAL Pro-Choice Maryland board member. STRONGEST FISCAL FINDING: pro-ESG - as delegate sponsored fossil-fuel divestment from the state pension system; as Comptroller / pension-board vice-chair drove the Dec 2024 Climate Advisory Panel. Voted for the 2021 Police Accountability Act (repealed LEOBR). Co-sponsored the 2018 red-flag/ERPO law (HB1302). Testified for barring county 287(g) ICE agreements (Community Trust Act). No cross-party deviation found. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Co-sponsored + voted for the 2022 Abortion Care Access Act expanding abortion access. Pro-choice.

1.note: Floor advocate for the reproductive-liberty constitutional amendment -> opposite of abolition.

4.note: Former Planned Parenthood intern and NARAL Pro-Choice Maryland board member; hosted 2022 event with former PP president Cecile Richards -> abortion-industry alignment.

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ (supported statewide LGBTQ Affairs commission). Does not affirm marriage as man-woman only.

1.note: Supports LGBTQ legal equality.

2.note: As Comptroller committed to update state systems for transgender/nonbinary name changes -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Supported LGBTQ commission and anti-discrimination legislation in policy.
Pro-ESG: sponsored fossil-fuel divestment of state pension funds as delegate; as Comptroller/pension VP-chair drove the Dec 2024 Climate Advisory Panel -> contrary to opposing ESG capture.

0.note: Voted for the 2021 Maryland Police Accountability Act, which repealed the Law Enforcement Officers' Bill of Rights -> contrary to affirmatively backing law-enforcement protections.

2.note: Testified for barring county 287(g) ICE agreements (Community Trust Act) -> pro-sanctuary, contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

0.note: Co-sponsored the 2018 red-flag/ERPO law -> opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Co-sponsored Extreme Risk Protective Orders (red-flag) law.

aaron-frey.notes_set: ME Attorney General since Jan 2019 (chosen by joint ballot of the Legislature). Re-selected Dec 4, 2024 for a term through Jan 2027; reported to be term-limited after this term, so the post-2026-election Legislature picks a successor. Former ME House (Bangor). Pro-abortion: joined multistate mifepristone-defense briefs; defended the 2024 abortion/gender-affirming-care shield law (called opponents' concerns 'meritless'). Pro-LGBTQ; defending Maine in the DOJ trans-athletes/Title IX suit (argues 'sex' includes gender identity). Carson v. Makin: after Maine lost at SCOTUS, moved to narrow the win via the Maine Human Rights Act to keep religious schools constrained ('not consistent with my reading of the Constitution'). Prolific anti-Trump-administration litigant; defending Maine's 72-hour gun waiting period. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (credited): OPPOSED the 2021 drug-decriminalization bill and broadly balked at progressive criminal-justice/harm-reduction reform -> public_justice q1 TRUE. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Joined multistate briefs defending mifepristone/abortion-pill access; pro-abortion-rights.

1.note: Defended 2024 abortion shield law; reaffirmed expanding abortion access -> opposite of abolition.

0.note: Joined 18-AG amicus supporting a teacher fired by a Catholic school over a same-sex marriage. Pro-LGBTQ.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage / LGBTQ legal equality.

2.note: Defending Maine on transgender athletes in girls'/women's sports; argues Title IX 'sex' includes gender identity -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Shield law protects gender-affirming-care providers; pro-LGBTQ policy posture.

0.note: After Carson v. Makin, moved to narrow Maine's tuition program via the Human Rights Act to keep religious schools constrained; called the school-choice ruling 'not consistent with my reading of the Constitution.'

1.note: Used state anti-discrimination law to keep religious schools out of public tuition funding -> adverse to religious conscience exemptions for Christian educators.
Joined 19-AG suit opposing the federal proof-of-citizenship voting EO -> contrary to photo voter ID with citizenship verification.
CROSS-PARTY: opposed the 2021 drug-decriminalization bill and balked at progressive criminal-justice/harm-reduction reform -> opposes a soft-on-crime measure. Documented Democrat deviation.
Backed suit to roll back ICE enforcement surge; pro-migrant -> contrary to mandatory deportation.

2.note: Anti-ICE-enforcement posture -> contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

4.note: Sued to block the EO ending birthright citizenship -> supports birthright citizenship, contrary to rubric.

0.note: Defending Maine's 72-hour firearm waiting period; argues 2A is not 'free from any regulation' -> opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Defends gun regulation (waiting period) in federal court.

dana-nessel.notes_set: MI Attorney General since Jan 2019 (reelected 2022). TERM-LIMITED: 2026 is her final year, not seeking reelection; successor race underway (D: McDonald/Savit/Totten; R: DePerno/Kijewski/Lloyd; primary Aug 4, 2026). Civil-rights attorney; was lead plaintiffs' counsel in DeBoer v. Snyder (the same-sex marriage case folded into Obergefell). Refused to enforce MI's 1931 abortion ban; defended Prop 3; joined mifepristone-defense coalitions. Sued to defend gender-affirming care for under-19 youth; 'a drag queen for every school' remark (2022). Challenged faith-based adoption agencies that decline same-sex couples (lost St. Vincent Catholic Charities case; state paid $550K fees); named defendant in Sacred Heart and St. Joseph parish religious-liberty suits. Pro-ESG/climate: Jan 2026 antitrust suit vs. fossil-fuel majors. Defended privately funded election grants (CTCL/'Zuckerbucks'). Testified for MI's 2023 red-flag/safe-storage/UBC laws and vowed to enforce against resistant sheriffs. Led the Feb 2025 14-state suit vs. the Musk/DOGE delegation; 45 suits vs. the second Trump administration in year one. No conservative-substance deviation found. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Refused to enforce MI's 1931 abortion ban; defended Prop 3 reproductive-freedom amendment. Pro-abortion-rights.

1.note: Joined multistate coalitions defending mifepristone -> opposite of abolition.

0.note: Lead plaintiffs' counsel in DeBoer v. Snyder challenging MI's same-sex marriage ban (folded into Obergefell). Does not affirm man-woman-only marriage.

1.note: Litigated to legalize same-sex marriage.

2.note: Sued to defend gender-affirming care for transgender youth -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: 'A drag queen for every school' remark (2022); pro-LGBTQ policy advocacy.

1.note: Sued to defend gender-affirming care for under-19 youth; called UM ending it possibly 'illegal' -> contrary to parental consent for minors' gender interventions.

4.note: Challenged faith-based adoption agencies declining same-sex couples -> contrary to supporting faith-based adoption agencies.

1.note: Actions narrowing conscience exemptions for faith institutions (faith-based adoption; joined challenge to ACA religious/moral contraceptive exemption; named defendant in Sacred Heart & St. Joseph parish suits).

4.note: Jan 2026 antitrust suit vs. fossil-fuel majors alleging suppression of renewables -> pro-energy-transition/ESG-aligned posture, contrary to opposing ESG capture. (SOFT)
Defended privately funded CTCL election grants ('Zuckerbucks') -> contrary to opposing private election funding.

1.note: Challenged the 'ideological deportation policy'; sued over immigration-conditioned funding -> contrary to mandatory deportation.

2.note: Backed courthouse civil-immigration-arrest limits; defended refugee resettlement -> contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

0.note: Testified for MI's 2023 red-flag/safe-storage/universal-background-check laws -> opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports red-flag laws; vowed to enforce against resistant sheriffs.
